nativeRange.habitat: Native to rice paddies, marshes, ditches, and low-lying seasonally-flooded fields across India and South-East Asia, where it grows as an annual marsh weed. Amphibious — tolerates both emersed (compact rounded leaves) and submersed (elongated stem) growth forms. Naturalised as a problematic rice-field weed in parts of Africa, Europe, and the southern United States (California, Louisiana).

decorPreferences: medium-to-high lighting for pink-red leaf tips
iron and full micronutrient dosing for coloration
open water column for tall stems

summary: The TRUE Rotala indica — an Indian/South-East Asian marsh stem plant with oval, cartilaginous-margined leaves that flush pink-red at the tips under bright light and iron dosing. Distinct from R. rotundifolia, which is widely mis-sold under this name.
careNotes: IMPORTANT TRADE-NAME CONFUSION: this entry covers the TRUE Rotala indica (Willd.) Koehne — a botanically distinct species from Rotala rotundifolia, which has historically been (and still is) sold under the trade name 'Rotala indica' or 'Rotala Indica Red' in the aquarium hobby. The two species are sister Rotalas in the same genus but differ visibly: R. indica has rounder, obovate leaves with thick whitish cartilaginous margins (per Wikipedia), grows somewhat shorter and bushier in tanks, and shows pink-red coloration mainly at the leaf tips (rather than across the whole stem as in highly-coloured R. rotundifolia cultivars). If you specifically want R. indica, buy from a vendor that labels its stock with the 'true' qualifier (e.g., Buce Plant's 'Rotala Indica True' SKU); generic 'Rotala Indica' from retailers is almost always R. rotundifolia. Native to India and South-East Asia, where it grows as an annual marsh and rice-paddy weed. Sources consistently report a tolerance range of 20-28°C (68-82°F), pH 6.0-7.5, and gH 2-12. KH 3-8 is reported by Aquariadise. Light demand is generally considered medium: 30-50 PAR per Aquariumbreeder; multiple sources note that brighter light + CO2 + iron dosing develops pink-red coloration in the upper stems and leaf tips. CO2 is optional and not strictly required — the plant grows reasonably in low-tech setups — but is widely advised for dense growth and reliable red colour. Adult height in submerged aquarium culture is typically 15-30 cm (Wikipedia documents up to 30-40 cm stem length in the wild; Aquariumsource's '1-2 feet' figure is an outlier and probably reflects emersed/pond growth). Tank minimum of 40 L (~10 gal) is the cross-source consensus floor for a background stem plant of this size. Care is broadly rated easy to beginner-intermediate; the plant is hardy across a wide parameter range but has fragile leaves that can be damaged by rough handling or aggressive tankmates. Like other Rotala species it dislikes drastic parameter swings and can melt if moved between very different water chemistries. Propagation is by stem cuttings (head cuttings of ~10 cm root quickly when replanted) and from lateral side shoots produced after trimming. Nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil) is preferred; the plant is a heavy nutrient scavenger and is sometimes used for nitrate/phosphate export in planted tanks. Avoid keeping with crayfish, freshwater crabs, or large herbivorous fish (goldfish, silver dollars, big cichlids) which will damage or eat the foliage.
breedingNotes: Propagated easily by stem cuttings — cut a 5-10 cm section of healthy stem above a leaf node, strip the lower leaves, and replant directly into nutrient-rich substrate. New roots and side shoots develop within days under good conditions. Trimming the apical bud encourages multiple lateral shoots from the mother stem, which can each be cut off and replanted to multiply the plant rapidly. Floating cuttings will also root in the water column before planting. The plant can be grown emersed (in which form it produces the rounder marsh leaves and small four-petalled pink flowers described in botanical references) and is occasionally propagated emersed in nurseries before being acclimated to submerged form for sale.

primary.name: Buce Plant
primary.notes: Original primary URL (buceplant.com/products/rotala-indica-true) returned 404 — swapped to the canonical Buce Plant Rotala indica product page (same vendor, same species). Used for family (Lythraceae / Loosestrife), origin (South-East Asia), height (5-12" ≈ 13-30 cm), light (medium), CO2 (recommended for red coloration), pH (6-7.5), growth rate (moderate, fast once established), placement (background), care (easy), propagation (stem cuttings + lateral shoots), and the note that leaves are fragile. The Buce Plant page does not itself distinguish 'true' R. indica from R. rotundifolia.

additional.name: Wikipedia
additional.notes: Taxonomic authority. Confirms scientific name Rotala indica (Willd.) Koehne, family Lythraceae, order Myrtales. Documents native range (South-East Asia, including India), wild stem height 30-40 cm, obovate leaves up to 2 cm with cartilaginous whitish margins, four pink petals, and status as a naturalised rice-field weed in Italy, Portugal, Spain, Congo, California, and Louisiana.

additional.name: Shrimp and Snail Breeder (aquariumbreeder.com)
additional.notes: Strong cross-check on water parameters and morphology: temperature 20-28 °C, pH 6.0-7.5, gH 2-12, light demand medium (30-50 PAR), CO2 not needed to low, fertilizers not needed to low, fast growth, height 10-20 cm in tanks, minimum tank 10 gal (~40 L), placement midground/background, care easy-to-medium, propagation by 10-cm stem cuttings. Also explicitly documents the morphological distinctions between R. indica (rounder/obovate leaves, smaller, solitary or spike inflorescence) and R. rotundifolia (narrow lanceolate leaves, larger, terminal inflorescence) — the basis for our 'TRUE indica' framing in careNotes.

additional.name: Aquariadise
additional.notes: Additional cross-check: temperature 22-28 °C (72-82 °F), pH 6.0-7.5, hardness 3-8 dKH, moderate-to-high light, CO2 optional, fast growth, midground/background placement, 10-gal minimum tank, easy care. Lists native countries Bangladesh, Thailand, India, Myanmar — source for the South Asia + South-East Asia country list.

additional.name: Aquarium Source
additional.notes: Used for KH range (3-8) and temperature confirmation (72-82 °F, ≈ 22-28 °C). Reports a much taller maximum height (1-2 feet) than other sources — treated as outlier reflecting emersed/pond growth and not used for adultSizeCm.
